House at the End of the Street (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $31,611,916Details
International Box Office $12,492,066
Worldwide Box Office $44,103,982
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,611,836
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,055,138
Total $10,666,974
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Seeking a fresh start, newly divorced Sarah and her daughter Elissa find the house of their dreams in a small, upscale, rural town. But when startling and unexplainable events begin to happen, Sarah and Elissa learn the town is in the shadows of a chilling secret. Years earlier, in the house next door, a daughter killed her parents in their beds, and disappeared - leaving only a brother, Ryan, as the sole survivor. Against Sarah's wishes, Elissa begins a relationship with the reclusive Ryan - and the closer they get, the deeper they're all pulled into a mystery more dangerous than they ever imagined.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,287,234 (38.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.57 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:71.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$6,900,000 (worldwide box office is 6.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,083 opening theaters/3,083 max. theaters, 4.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $47,497,298

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 21st, 2012 (Wide) by Relativity
International Releases: September 21st, 2012 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
September 27th, 2012 (Wide) (Argentina)
September 28th, 2012 (Wide) (Mexico)
October 3rd, 2012 (Wide) (Egypt)
October 4th, 2012 (Wide) (Croatia)
Video Release: January 8th, 2013 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of violence and terror, thematic elements, language, some teen partying and brief drug material.
(Rating bulletin 2186, 8/26/2011)
Running Time: 101 minutes
Plot point: Children Dealing with Divorce, Cross-Dressing, Death of a Sibling, Dysfunctional Family, Kidnap, Mental Illness, Mistaken Identity, Orphan, Serial Killer, Surprise Twist, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Release detail: Delayed Release

Subgenre: Psychological Horror

Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Relativity Media, FilmNation Entertainment, A Bigger Boat Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Seeking a fresh start, newly divorced Sarah and her daughter Elissa find the house of their dreams in a small, upscale, rural town. But when startling and unexplainable events begin to happen, Sarah and Elissa learn the town is in the shadows of a chilling secret. Years earlier, in the house next door, a daughter killed her parents in their beds, and disappeared - leaving only a brother, Ryan, as the sole survivor. Against Sarah's wishes, Elissa begins a relationship with the reclusive Ryan - and the closer they get, the deeper they're all pulled into a mystery more dangerous than they ever imagined.
